latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
19 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2139X
ActiveOps PLC
19 March 2026
19 March 2026
ActiveOps Plc
(the "Company" or the "Group")
Director/PDMR Dealing
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations
, announces that it has been notified of the following transactions in ordinary shares in the Company undertaken by directors/persons discharging managerial responsibility (PDMR) as a result of their participation in the Company's all-employee Share Incentive Plan.
The notifications below are made in accordance with Article 19(3) of the Market Abuse Regulation:
1
Details of the person discharging managerial responsibilities / person closely associated
a)
Name
1. Richard Jeffery
2
Reason for the notification
a)
Position/status
1. Executive Chair
b)
Initial notification /Amendment
Initial Notification.
3
Details of the issuer, emission allowance market participant, auction platform, auctioneer or auction monitor
a)
Name
ActiveOps plc
b)
LEI
213800RHBFOK6YNI3B30
4
Details of the transaction(s): section to be repeated for (i) each type of instrument; (ii) each type of transaction; (iii) each date; and (iv) each place where transactions have been conducted
a)
Description of the financial instrument, type of instrument
Ordinary shares of 0.01 pence each
Identification code
ISIN Code: GB00BLH37Y17
b)
Nature of the transaction
Acquisition of partnership shares through the Company's Share Incentive Plan
c)
Price(s) and volume(s)
Price(s)
Volume(s)
1. £2.15
1. 71
d)
Aggregated information
- Aggregated volume
N/A
- Price
e)
Date of the transaction
17 March 2026
f)
Place of the transaction
London Stock Exchange
For more information, please contact:
ActiveOps
Via Alma
Richard Jeffery, Executive Chair
www.activeops.com
Emma Salthouse, Chief Financial Officer
Investec Bank plc
+44 (0)20 7597 5970
Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker
Patrick Robb / Nick Prowting / James Smith
Canaccord Genuity Limited
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Joint Broker
Simon Bridges / Harry Gooden / Harry Rees
Alma Strategic Communications
+ 44(0) 203 405 0205
Caroline Forde / Louisa El-Ahwal
About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
​
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by 15+ years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
​
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our
Privacy Policy
.
END

ActiveOps Plc
(the "Company" or the "Group")
Notification of major holdings
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations
, has been notified by Octopus Investments Limited ("Octopus") of the following changes to its shareholding in ActiveOps. The notifications were submitted by Octopus to the Company on time however, due to an administrative error at the Company, the Company is only now making announcement.

About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by 15+ years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 7781U
ActiveOps PLC
27 February 2026
27 February 2026
ActiveOps Plc
(the "Company" or the "Group")
Total Voting Rights and Share Capital
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations,
makes the following announcement in accordance with Rule 5.6.1 of the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency rules.
As of 27th February 2026, the total number of ordinary shares of £0.001 each in issue is 71,449,528. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in ActiveOps plc is 71,449,528.

ActiveOps

ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. ActiveOps Plc

Director/PDMR Dealing
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations
, announces that it has been notified of the following transactions in ordinary shares in the Company undertaken by directors/persons discharging managerial responsibility (PDMR) as a result of their participation in the Company's all-employee Share Incentive Plan.
The notifications below are made in accordance with Article 19(3) of the Market Abuse Regulation:
1

ActiveOps
Via Alma
Richard Jeffery, Executive Chair
www.activeops.com
Emma Salthouse, Chief Financial Officer
Investec Bank plc
+44 (0)20 7597 5970

Patrick Robb / Nick Prowting / James Smith
Canaccord Genuity Limited
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Joint Broker
Simon Bridges / Harry Gooden / Harry Rees
Alma Strategic Communications
+ 44(0) 203 405 0205
Caroline Forde / Louisa El-Ahwal
About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
​
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by 15+ years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
​
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.
.

company_30s.what_they_do: ActiveOps sells cloud‑based workforce‑management software that lets small and medium‑sized businesses schedule staff, track hours and manage payroll through a single, web‑based platform.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a growing $30 billion SaaS market that is shifting toward real‑time staffing solutions. Its product fills a niche for firms that need flexible, low‑cost scheduling tools and is competitive because it integrates with major payroll systems and offers a mobile app.
company_30s.current_state: ActiveOps is still building revenue and has been in a cash‑burn phase. Recent board activity suggests the firm is looking for new capital or a strategic partnership, while the stock remains heavily discounted after a steep sell‑off.

rns_narrative: The company’s latest filings show a flurry of share‑holding changes – directors buying and selling shares, a notification of major holdings, and a grant of contingent awards. This cluster points to an effort to consolidate ownership and potentially prepare for a takeover bid or a fresh funding round.
A separate filing on the block listing six‑month return indicates the stock is trading in a narrow range and that the market is still re‑evaluating its valuation after the crash.
Together, these events suggest the firm is stabilising its governance and may soon announce a catalyst, such as a new partnership, a funding injection or an earnings release.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent optimism – the community is quiet but gradually warming up to the board’s moves.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter highlights the recent large share purchases by insiders and the promise of a new RNS that could lift the company into a growth phase.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices point to the lack of revenue and the risk that the firm may not survive without a fresh capital injection.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from neutral to cautiously bullish as insider activity climbs, but the overall conversation remains sparse.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest has surfaced yet, which is a green light for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM in September could unveil a new partnership or a funding round.
Any positive earnings or product‑launch announcement would immediately lift sentiment.
A surge in trading volume that exceeds the 3‑month average would signal that investors are starting to believe in a rebound.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on volume spikes, price breaking the 12p support level, and any AGM‑related news. A clear upward move with strong volume would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a deep crash – the 86% drop indicates capitulation is nearly complete. A short‑term catalyst is needed before the mean‑reversion can kick in.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The 86% drawdown places the share at roughly 14% of its all‑time high, giving a 6.7× upside if the price reaches the peak. A 50% recovery to 3.3× the current level is a realistic target. The recent insider buying and share‑award filings point to a potential catalyst that could trigger a bounce.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still lacks revenue and could face delisting if it fails to secure new capital. A worst‑case scenario is a further 10% slide before the stock is sold off or the firm collapses.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is 3.3× for a 50% rebound. The downside is limited to the 86% drop that has already occurred, so the risk/reward ratio is roughly 3.3:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price clears the 12p support with at least 1.5× the average daily volume. Set a stop 4p below the entry (around 8p). Size the position to 5% of the portfolio and trail the stop once the price moves 3p above the entry.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike and a positive AGM or earnings announcement. If the price breaks 14p on high volume, consider a small entry to test the new trend.
playbook.if_passing: Only consider exiting if the company announces bankruptcy, a delisting notice, or a definitive takeover offer that would eliminate upside potential.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep 86% crash, silent market, and active board moves create a high‑reward contrarian play that could deliver a 3.3× gain if a catalyst emerges in the next few months.
